title: NAMES — baby/character name shortlist + 'name origin' criteria
html:
NAMES

Source: 2023-07-23 Apple Note, last modified 2026-03-15 (kept open and added-to for ~2.5 years). Two clusters: (1) a baby/character name shortlist Yahir likes, and (2) a meta-list of "interesting name-origin circumstances" — what makes a name memorable independent of the spelling.

TL;DR

  • Name shortlist (gender-flexible, leaning Latin/Mediterranean/Slavic): Jrei, Giovanni, Jovani, Jordana, Georgina, Dorian, Napoleone, Marvin, Gilbert, Jon ___, Luci, Sailor, Gina, Jana, Zora, Avari
  • "What makes a name origin interesting" meta-list: chose own name, born on a random ass island/country, born on a boat, godfather is a random celeb or stranger, name has the ü umlaut
  • The umlaut criterion is the tell — Yahir is interested in diacritic-as-identity-marker, not just sound
  • This note was kept open for 30+ months — a long-running canvas, not a one-off jot. The manifesto style note genre, but for naming
  • Sits alongside the July 2024 mega notebook which name-drops fictional/meme characters Lajarius, Donezzetti, Ducleus — same name-curiosity instinct, different mode

Pattern observed

Yahir thinks about names the way a novelist does — not "what should we call the kid," but "what's the story of the name." The "born on a boat" and "godfather is a random celebrity" criteria are story seeds, not naming criteria. This is the same impulse that produces his meme name log and the character-naming in his half-pitched stealth app concepts.

Open questions

  • Are any of these names tied to a specific person Yahir has met (Zora, Jordana, Avari)?
  • What's the through-line — would he actually name a child Sailor, or is this aesthetic curation only?

Related

  • 2023 12 28 meme name
  • lineage as fate
  • wrld brand suffix
